mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance after 2012.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or interaction term coefficient becomes insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during mid-cycle expansions, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5 percent monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in 65 percent of months following CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4 percent, or the negative correlation vanishes after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with falling unemployment confirms robust labor demand, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 150 thousand while UNRATE declines
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in 55 percent of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent confirms sustained economic expansion, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in 60 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0 percent, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising policy rates amid elevated inflation signal a restrictive monetary stance that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.0 percent annualized
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in 60 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS increases alongside CPIAUCSL above 2.0 percent, or the joint condition fails to predict direction in rolling tests.
